metadata.name: Vandalblast — normal cast then overloaded cast
metadata.description: Tests both modes of Vandalblast. Scenario 1 (normal): p1 casts Vandalblast for {R} targeting p2's Sol Ring. Sol Ring is destroyed; p1's Mind Stone survives. Scenario 2 (overload): p1 casts Vandalblast with overload for {4}{R} (cast_spell_overload). ALL artifacts p1 doesn't control are destroyed — p2's Arcane Signet and p2's Thought Vessel are both destroyed. P1's Mind Stone survives because overloaded Vandalblast reads 'destroy each artifact you don't control' (CR 702.96b: no targets when overloaded; CR 702.96c: 'target' replaced by 'each'). This demonstrates the defining property of Overload: the caster's own permanents are exempt.
metadata.generation_notes: Vandalblast oracle text: '{R}, Sorcery — Destroy target artifact you don't control. Overload {4}{R} (You may cast this spell for its overload cost. If you do, change its text by replacing all instances of "target" with "each".)'. Card definition (vandalblast.rs): mana_cost {R}, AbilityDefinition::Overload { cost: ManaCost { generic: 4, red: 1 } }, AbilityDefinition::Spell with Effect::Conditional { condition: Condition::WasOverloaded, if_true: ForEach over EachPermanentMatching(Artifact, Opponent), if_false: DestroyPermanent(DeclaredTarget{0}) }. Normal cast: single target, destroys one artifact not controlled by caster. Overload cast: no targets (CR 702.96b), text becomes 'destroy each artifact you don't control' (CR 702.96c). CR 702.96a: overload cost {4}{R} replaces the base mana cost {R}. Scenario 1: p1 controls Mind Stone (survives), p2 controls Sol Ring (destroyed). Scenario 2: p1 still controls Mind Stone (survives); p2 now controls Arcane Signet and Thought Vessel (both destroyed). Mana pool pre-loaded for both casts: scenario 1 needs {R}, scenario 2 needs {4}{R}, total = red:2 + colorless:4. Both scenarios share the same initial state; mana is consumed sequentially across script steps. Key assertion: p1's Mind Stone remains on battlefield in both scenarios, confirming the 'you don't control' restriction.

script.step_note: Scenario 1: p1 casts Vandalblast normally for {R} targeting p2's Sol Ring

actions.description: Initial: p1 has Mind Stone; p2 has Sol Ring, Arcane Signet, Thought Vessel; p1 has 2x Vandalblast in hand

actions.note: CR 601.2: p1 casts Vandalblast normally for {R}, targeting p2's Sol Ring (an artifact p1 doesn't control).

actions.description: After normal cast: Vandalblast on stack, p1 hand down to 1
actions.note: Vandalblast moved from hand to stack.
CR 116.3: Both players pass priority — Vandalblast resolves.

resolution.note: CR 701.7a: Sol Ring is destroyed and moves to p2's graveyard.

actions.note: CR 608.2: Vandalblast resolves. Normal mode destroys the single targeted artifact (Sol Ring). Mind Stone is unaffected.

actions.description: After normal Vandalblast: Sol Ring gone, p1's Mind Stone survives, stack empty
actions.note: Normal Vandalblast destroyed only Sol Ring (its single target). P1's Mind Stone survived — the 'you don't control' condition prevented p1 from targeting their own artifacts.

script.step_note: Scenario 2: p1 casts Vandalblast with overload for {4}{R} — destroys ALL artifacts p1 doesn't control

actions.description: Before overloaded cast: p1 has 1 Vandalblast in hand, Mind Stone on battlefield; p2 has Arcane Signet and Thought Vessel

actions.note: CR 702.96a: p1 pays the overload cost {4}{R} instead of the base cost {R}. CR 702.96b: the spell has no targets — it affects each artifact p1 doesn't control. Total mana paid: {4}{R}.

actions.description: After overloaded cast: Vandalblast on stack, p1 hand empty
actions.note: Overloaded Vandalblast is on the stack. No targets were declared (CR 702.96b).
CR 116.3: Both players pass priority — overloaded Vandalblast resolves.

resolution.note: CR 702.96c: Overload's text-changing effect replaced 'target' with 'each', so the spell reads 'destroy each artifact you don't control.' CR 702.96b: The spell had no targets. P1's Mind Stone is NOT destroyed because it is controlled by p1. Both of p2's artifacts (Arcane Signet, Thought Vessel) are destroyed simultaneously.

actions.note: CR 608.2: Overloaded Vandalblast resolves. The text-changing effect (CR 702.96c) makes it hit every artifact p1 doesn't control simultaneously.

actions.description: Final: p2's artifacts destroyed, p1's Mind Stone survives, stack empty
actions.note: Overloaded Vandalblast destroyed all of p2's artifacts (Arcane Signet and Thought Vessel). P1's Mind Stone survived — it is controlled by p1, the caster, so the 'you don't control' restriction exempts it (CR 702.96b + 702.96c).
